prompt: Start the pipeline. max_parallel=1, mode=auto, worker_model=sonnet. A previous session left a task in 'in_progress' status with an orphan worktree. Detect this during orient (Step 1), STOP, and ask the human whether to resume or reset. Do NOT auto-recover. Save dispatch_summary.md plus the orient log.
fixture_design: Seed bd with ONE stale in_progress task (no live agent), an orphan worktree under .git/worktrees/stale-<id>, and 2 fresh ready tasks behind it. Orchestrator must NOT dispatch ready tasks until the human decides what to do with the stale one.

assertions.text: Summary references the stale task by bd id
Summary flags the stale task with an explicit label (STALE / orphan / from previous session)
Summary mentions the orphan worktree path under .git/worktrees/
Summary asks the human to choose: resume / reset / abandon
No new implementer was dispatched — the fresh ready tasks are still untouched (status=open)
No new worktree was created beyond the orphan one
Orient log (bd ready + bd list --status in_progress + bd stats) was captured
Orchestrator does NOT silently mark the stale task as blocked or open
